ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the user */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the user */,
  "subscription_plan" TEXT /* Subscription plan of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "release_year" INTEGER /* Release year of the movie */,
  "director" TEXT /* Director of the movie */,
  "cast" TEXT /* Main cast of the movie */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the movie in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who gave the rating */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being rated */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (e.g., from 0 to 5) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the rating was given */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "parent_genre" INTEGER /* Parent genre of the current genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre associated with the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_preferences" (
  "preference_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each preference entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the preferred genre */,
  "preference_strength" REAL /* Strength of the user's preference for the genre (e.g., from 0 to 1) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("preference_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_preferences_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_preferences_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "viewing_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each viewing record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who watched the movie */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie that was watched */,
  "view_date" TEXT /* Date when the movie was watched */,
  "watch_time" INTEGER /* Time spent watching the movie in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_viewing_history_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_viewing_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recommendations" (
  "recommendation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recommendation */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the recommendation */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recommended movie */,
  "algorithm_used" TEXT /* Recommendation algorithm used to generate the recommendation */,
  "recommended_date" TEXT /* Date when the recommendation was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recommendation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recommendations_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recommendations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the campaign */,
  "campaign_budget" REAL /* Budget for the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_engagement" (
  "engagement_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engagement record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the engagement record */,
  "engagement_score" REAL /* Engagement score based on user activity */,
  "engagement_type" TEXT /* Type of engagement (e.g., likes, comments, shares) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engagement_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_engagement_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
